codeReview.summary: The worker failure isolation feature is fully implemented and thoroughly tested. All 7 focus areas are correctly handled: error propagation via parent-child channels, parent task resilience through context isolation, task cancellation with ownership validation, runtime health maintenance, sibling worker isolation, proper cleanup on failure, and recovery after restart.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker implemented a critical pattern for context persistence during shutdown that should be documented as a project convention. When a task's context is cancelled (due to runtime shutdown or task cancellation), all persistence operations (store updates, event emission, channel notifications) must use context.Background() instead of the cancelled context. This ensures state is properly persisted even during shutdown. This pattern appears in handleExecutionError at internal/runtime/runtime.go:726-758.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/runtime/runtime.go:726-758 - The persistCtx := context.Background() pattern ensures store.UpdateTask, emitEvent, FailAgentMutation, updateWorkItemState, and notifyParent all complete successfully even when the original ctx is cancelled.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The go-worker skill documentation correctly captures the feature scope and work procedure. The skill mentions 'Runtime tool loop and channel updates' which accurately covers this feature's channel-based parent-child communication. The handoff confirms followedProcedure=true with no deviations.
No new services needed to be added for this feature. The POST /api/agent/cancel endpoint is properly integrated into the existing runtime API handler registration at internal/runtime/api.go:523.

The library documentation at .factory/library/worker-failure-isolation.md is comprehensive and well-structured, covering all 3 validation assertions (VAL-CHOIR-009, VAL-CHOIR-010, VAL-CHOIR-014). The document correctly explains the context persistence fix and the failure notification flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/worker-failure-isolation.md - Contains complete implementation details for CancelTask, POST /api/agent/cancel endpoint, context persistence fix, and test coverage summary.

summary: Feature scheduler-worker-failure-isolation is fully implemented with 24 comprehensive tests covering all aspects of worker failure isolation, task cancellation, and recovery. The implementation correctly: (1) propagates errors from failed children to parents via channels, (2) keeps parent tasks running after child failures, (3) implements task cancellation with proper ownership validation, (4) maintains runtime health during partial failures, (5) isolates sibling workers from failures, (6) cleans up state properly on failure, and (7) recovers interrupted tasks on restart. No blocking or non-blocking issues found. The worker followed skill procedures correctly.
